originalTitle: The ‘G-Wagen of golf carts’ could be the ideal second car
summary: While the auto industry wrings its hands over the electric vehicle market, sweating details like aerodynamic efficiency and range anxiety, a new EV startup based in Lisbon, Portugal, is zagging in a different direction. Amble's new electric buggy won't impress anyone with its 0-60 time or its self-driving features (it has none). Instead, it takes […]
content: While the auto industry wrings its hands over the electric vehicle market, sweating details like aerodynamic efficiency and range anxiety, a new EV startup based in Lisbon, Portugal, is zagging in a different direction. Amble’s new electric buggy won’t impress anyone with its 0–60 time or its self-driving features (it has none). Instead, it takes a stab at the belief that cars have gotten too big, too fast, and perhaps could use a bit of a downgrade in both departments. The Amble One is a premium, street-legal buggy with a gorgeous neo-retro design that’s currently being marketed to locations where traditional cars are unnecessary or too large, like hotels or resorts. Developed by a team of alumni from Apple, Audi, and Cowboy, the compact EV is intended to be the solution for a society overrun by oversized, overpowered trucks and SUVs. And it could be coming to a neighborhood near you. Rather than competing directly with conventional automobiles, Amble wanted to create an entirely new category of vehicle intended to become a household’s second vehicle rather than its primary car, CEO Adrien Roose told me. “If we look at the stats, most European and American families have, like, two cars on average,” Roose said. “The reality is that there is one car that brings the emotional freedom or the actual freedom that you need to go on a highway and to go on vacation or to go on long weekends. But the reality is that the second vehicle is mostly used for, like, school runs and grocery shoppings and neighborhoods.” He added, “We want to be the ultimate solution for these short trips.” [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Amble-0085_HERO.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Amble-0048.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Amble-0052.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Amble-0113.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Amble-0140.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Amble-0312.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Amble-0305.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Amble-0293.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] The Amble One is open and minimal, but with a sturdy design perfect for off-road trails and beach paths. Built as an open-air vehicle with no doors, it features folding front seats, a digital display, and physical controls utilizing leather and cork befitting of its Portuguese origins. There’s also a front cargo rack for luggage or other storage, and the rear seats fold flat for surfboards or other gear. Built-in mounts lets you add baskets, straps, mirrors, and other accessories. With a 15 kW motor and an 11kWh battery, it won’t win any drag or long-distance races. The Amble One caps out at 40 mph (65 km/h) and can travel upwards of 62 miles (100 km) on a single charge. It can fill up its battery from a standard wall outlet in five hours and has a curb weight of under 450 kg (992 lbs). Roose described weight as one of the company’s greatest engineering challenges because the vehicle is being homologated under Europe’s L7e quadricycle regulations. To qualify, the complete vehicle—including the battery—must weigh less than 450 kilograms. Its a truly demanding target, with Roose describing it as the source of some of the team’s most intense engineering debates. The company is beginning with a resort-oriented vehicle, but Roose says it’s been intentionally engineered for local roads as well. Looking further ahead, the ambition is to introduce a broader family of low-speed vehicles that collectively address short-distance transportation. During our conversation, Roose showed me a rendering of a version of the vehicle with doors and windows that looked like a cross between a dune buggy and a Mini Moke. But Roose would prefer a more upscale comparison than the breezy runabout. “The inspiration for this vehicle comes initially from the Lunar Rover, but they also looked into older 4x4 like the G-Wagen, which is very famous for its very flat surfaces and other such vehicles,” Roose said. “Some people could call it a glorified golf cart, but it’s really in between in terms of platform. If you look at the battery, it’s three to four times the range of a golf cart.” [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/240701_AMBLE_iii0547.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/240701_AMBLE_iiii0101.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/240701_AMBLE_iiii0132.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/240701_AMBLE_iiii0258.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/240701_AMBLE0051.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/240701_AMBLE0188.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/240701_AMBLE0299.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/240701_AMBLE0694.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/240701_AMBLE0703.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/260603_Amble_Urban_045.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/streetlegal_EU_yellow.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/streetlegal_US.jpg?quali
chineseTitle: “高尔夫球车中的 G-Wagen”可能是理想的第二辆车
chineseSummary: 当汽车行业在电动汽车市场上绞尽脑汁、为空气动力学效率和里程焦虑等细节而苦恼时，一家总部位于葡萄牙里斯本的新电动汽车初创公司却正朝着不同的方向前进。 Amble 的新型电动越野车不会以其 0-60 时间或自动驾驶功能（它没有）给任何人留下深刻的印象。相反，它需要[...]
chineseContent: 当汽车行业在电动汽车市场上绞尽脑汁、为空气动力学效率和里程焦虑等细节而苦恼时，一家总部位于葡萄牙里斯本的新电动汽车初创公司却正朝着不同的方向前进。 Amble 的新型电动越野车不会以其 0-60 时间或自动驾驶功能（它没有）给任何人留下深刻的印象。相反，它挑战了人们的信念，即汽车变得太大、太快，也许可以在这两个部门进行一些降级。 Amble One 是一款可合法上路的优质越野车，采用华丽的新复古设计，目前正在销售到不需要传统汽车或太大的地方，例如酒店或度假村。这款紧凑型电动汽车由苹果、奥迪和牛仔的校友团队开发，旨在成为超大型、动力过大的卡车和 SUV 泛滥的社会的解决方案。它可能会来到你附近的社区。首席执行官阿德里安·鲁斯 (Adrien Roose) 告诉我，Amble 不想直接与传统汽车竞争，而是希望创造一种全新的汽车类别，旨在成为家庭的第二辆车，而不是主要汽车。 “如果我们看一下统计数据，大多数欧洲和美国家庭平均拥有两辆车，”罗斯说。 “现实情况是，有一辆车可以带来情感上的自由或实际的自由，让你可以在高速公路上、去度假或度过长周末。但现实情况是，第二辆车主要用于学校跑步、杂货店购物和社区活动。”他补充道，“我们希望成为这些短途旅行的最终解决方案。” [图片：https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Amble-0085_HERO.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [图片：https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/A
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
